articles.title: Vanguard Group Inc. Buys 971,773 Shares of Starbucks Corporation $SBUX
articles.summary: Vanguard Group Inc. increased its stake in Starbucks Corporation by 0.9%, purchasing an additional 971,773 shares to reach a total of 114,410,675 shares, representing a 10.06% position valued at approximately $9.63 billion. This comes despite Starbucks missing Q earnings per share estimates but exceeding revenue expectations, and announcing a quarterly dividend with a high payout ratio. The article also notes recent insider selling and various analyst ratings and market sentiments surrounding SBUX.

articles.title: Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) Is Up 10.1% After Mixed Q1 And JDE Peet’s Deal - Has The Bull Case Changed?
articles.summary: Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) reported mixed Q1 2026 results with increased sales but lower net income, alongside its acquisition of JDE Peet’s and renewed Starbucks K-Cup agreements. The company's investment narrative focuses on balancing its North American beverage strength with its coffee business, noting that coffee integration and pricing are key catalysts, while cost and tariff pressures remain risks. Despite a wide range of fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community, forecasts project significant revenue and earnings growth by 2029, suggesting a potential 21% upside from its current price.

articles.title: Mattern Capital Management LLC Sells 98,055 Shares of Starbucks Corporation $SBUX
articles.summary: Mattern Capital Management LLC significantly reduced its stake in Starbucks Corporation, selling 98,055 shares, a 93.5% decrease, leaving them with 6,834 shares valued at $575,000. Insider sales also occurred with CEO Brady Brewer and EVP Sara Kelly selling shares, bringing total insider combined sales over the last 90 days to 6,958 shares. This comes as Starbucks missed EPS estimates but beat revenue expectations, and several institutional investors adjusted their holdings.

articles.title: Starbucks Corporation $SBUX Shares Bought by Teacher Retirement System of Texas
articles.summary: The Teacher Retirement System of Texas increased its stake in Starbucks (SBUX) by 16.6% during the fourth quarter, now holding 204,473 shares valued at over $17.2 million. Institutional investors collectively own about 72.29% of Starbucks' stock. Wall Street analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for Starbucks, with an average price target of $104.75, following recent earnings that missed EPS but beat revenue estimates, and the announcement of a $0.62 quarterly dividend.

articles.title: Five things to know about this week's massive Starbucks announcement
articles.summary: Starbucks made one of the largest job announcements in Nashville's history on April 21st. This article highlights key information regarding Starbucks' expansion, focusing on the establishment of a new corporate office and the creation of approximately 2,000 new jobs in the area. It also mentions that Starbucks is looking to fill six-figure roles with local talent and has chosen a Gulch spot for an interim office.

articles.title: Stifel Boosts Starbucks (SBUX) Target, Sees Earnings In Line with Estimates
articles.summary: Stifel analyst Chris O’Cull raised Starbucks' (SBUX) price target to $115 from $105, maintaining a Buy rating ahead of the company's earnings report. The firm anticipates Starbucks' earnings per share to align with the Street's estimate of 42 cents, citing solid domestic sales. Starbucks also announced a $100 million investment to establish a corporate office in Nashville, creating 2,000 support jobs and relocating some Seattle teams.
